moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce broad index exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income focus, addressing its trim flag by increasing allocation.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to manage single-name concentration.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into a new, less correlated sector, improving overall portfolio stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but will likely limit upside potential. Expect lower volatility, but drawdowns are still possible in broad market declines.

moves.note: Press strength in top quality tech

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and manage broad index exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove high-risk leveraged ETFs and reduce overall leverage.
Reduce QQQI to 15% to address its trim flag and rebalance income exposure.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a strong performing tech name.
Buy RTX at 10% to add diversification with a high-scoring scanner setup.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still seeking growth opportunities. While offering a blend of stability and potential upside, market downturns can still lead to drawdowns.

moves.note: Press strength in top quality tech

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free up capital for higher-conviction plays.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stops and reallocate capital to stronger setups.
Increase CRWD to 20% to aggressively lean into a high-scoring, uptrending tech name.
Buy RTX at 15% as a new, high-conviction long setup from the scanner.
Buy AMAT at 10% to add another strong semiconductor setup, diversifying within tech.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It focuses on leveraging strong scanner setups and pressing winning positions, but comes with significantly higher risk. Expect larger potential drawdowns and be aware that leveraged ETFs, if used, experience daily decay and rebalancing, amplifying losses in adverse conditions. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and reallocate to income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with an income strategy.
Increase QQQI to 40% to significantly boost income generation and address its trim flag.
Sell CRWD to remove high-beta single-name exposure, favoring stability.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to further decrease growth-oriented exposure, focusing on income.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It significantly reduces growth-oriented assets and leverage, focusing on income ETFs. While aiming for lower drawdown and reliable income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in market sell-offs; this is not a savings account.
